name: Short-term Spoken Chinese: Threshold (汉语口语速成·入门篇)
description: Mandarin vocabulary from Short-term Spoken Chinese, Threshold level (Ma Jianfei et al.; BLCU Press). Hand-entered as a study technique.

sources.name: Short-term Spoken Chinese (汉语口语速成)

field_values.value: "You're welcome."/"Don't mention it."/[lit. no good manners/politeness]
"Don't mention it"/"That's okay"/"It doesn't matter" [lit. no connection/relationship]
"What (kind of) tea (do you want to) drink?"
to have met/to know/(to meet/see for the first time)
to come; (used before a verb to inducate intended or suggested action (e.g. will, let, gunna, etc.))
(indicates that an action is brief or slight)
to see/look at/read; to visit/call on
wǒ yě hěn gāoxìng rènshi nǐ
"So-so." [lit. horse horse tiger tiger] (do not use--can sound terrible)
to have; to exist; to possess
"No it isn't." (said to a compliment; less formal, more used than the other) [lit. "Don't have, don't have."]
to be called/named; to call/name; to greet; to let
expensive; honourable (very polite way of asking for surname)
to want; to miss (somebody); to think (about)
(measure word for people, usually just for families)
(particle indicating a question suggesting confirmation or uncertainty--sometimes like Japanese "ne")
九点差一刻/差一刻九点
X hé Y yǒu shénme bùtóng?
to get up/get out of bed
nǐ jiā fùjìn yǒu shénme fànguǎn?
"What are the restaurants near your house?"
(counter for small round things, medicine, grapes, rice grains, candy)
zero; and (used like a decimal place in prices)
to return/go back; to call back
X多少钱(#counter), used more for discrete objects
"How much are the X?", used more for measured amounts
waitress; Miss [lit. little older sister]; prostitute (some places/contexts--be careful)
(measure word for shops, stores, or other places that make money)
今天我没有时间，你明天来吧。
to open; to bloom; to leave/start (train bus); to switch on; to hold a meeting
请你关门，我很冷。这家商店关门了，我们去别家吧。
请你关门，我很冷。这家商店关门了，我们去别家吧。
know (a skill);  be able to; can
to admire/envy (always in a positive sense)
to love; to like to do (used with verb or family/lover)
not so great/not up to much/not much to say
他的英语怎么样？不怎么样。
(indicate something just happened; indicate something is relatively late)
我家离Whole Foods很近，所以我常常去那儿买东西。
明天是妈妈的生日，我准备做一个蛋糕给她。下午要上课，我要准备一下儿。
准备好了吗 好了 没有
similar/about the same; almost; (just about right?)
(a measure word for number of actions/times (to completion))
I have never V until now (but...)
从来不V I have never V (period)...
to be on a business trip
nǐ zài chūzhōng de shíhou xué le dōucháng shíjiān yīngyǔ?
How long did you study English in Junior High?
你在初中的时候学了都长时间英语。
wǒ zài běijīng dàxué xué yīngyǔ.
I study English at Beijing University.
(after adjective, noun, or phrase to create adverbial adjunct)
hello (use when answer a phone call)
to remember; to keep in mind
电影开始了。我从明天开始工作。
to knock down; to run over
reasonable; proper (often used in negative/rhetorical questions)
to lose/misplace (an item); to put aside
to hate to do/part with X
美国人非常喜欢狗，中国人非常喜欢吃狗肉。
我想去的国家很多，比如英国和日本。
这个部分我还没学，我不知道。
这儿的人我差不多都认识。我有差不多20个学生。
这个购物中心的人太多了。
你打电话给我吧。没问题！
"like that"/"in that case"/"in that way"; then
你不喜欢他，那么为什么跟他去看电影？
我什么时候可以取照片？ 明天。
如果你会日语，那么汉语很容易。白色的衣服很容易脏。
这件衣服颜色太深了，不好看。
昨天他买了一束花给我，我很高兴。
他送给我的礼物很特别。
早上我一般吃面包、喝咖啡。
我们一起送礼物给他吧。
他每天都游泳，身体很好。
我正在吃饭，你一会儿再打电话给我吧。
